title: Samantha Cunliffe
content:
Partner | Co-Head, National Infrastructure Group
People.ContactEmail scunliffe@mccarthy.ca
- British Columbia, 2009
- University of British Columbia
Samantha delivers comprehensive legal advice to clients on complex infrastructure projects, from development through completion.
Samantha Cunliffe* is a Partner in the Business Law Group in Vancouver and Co-Head of our National Infrastructure Group. She provides creative solutions to clients with respect to the development, financing, commercial and other aspects of large-scale complex infrastructure projects across Canada.
Clients benefit from Samantha's versatile experience, as she regularly acts for developers, investors, contractors, and lenders on all aspects of projects in a wide range of industry sectors, including water treatment, social infrastructure, digital infrastructure, rail and other transportation.

### Recent representative work includes:
- Advising the successful bidder on the High Speed Rail Project
- Advising the lenders of the successful bidder on the Alberta Schools Bundle #5 Project
- Advising the successful bidder on the Go Rail Expansion On-Corridor Works Project
- Advising the successful bidder on the Finch West LRT Project
- Advising the lenders to the successful bidder on the Gordie Howe International Bridge Project
- Advising the lenders to the successful bidder on the CHEO Hospital Project
- Advising the lenders to the successful bidder on the Bayers Lake Community Outpatient Centre Project
- Advising the successful bidder on the Edmonton Valley Line West Project
- Advising the lenders to the successful bidder on the West Park Hospital Project
- Advising the lenders to the successful bidder on the Royal Inland Hospital Project
- Advising the lenders to the successful bidder on the Surrey-Langley Skytrain Project
- Advising the subordinated lender on its investment in the University of Toronto Retrofits Project
- Advising a developer on the financing and refinancing of various fibre projects across Canada
- Advising a client in the agriculture sector on the financing of a large plant
- Advising the lenders in connection with the financing of the Manitoba Fibre Project
- Advising the lenders on the Rock Networks Fibre Project
- Advising the lender on the Port Stalashen Wastewater Treatment Plant Project
- Advising the lender on the financing of two water and wastewater projects in Alberta and Ontario
Samantha joined the Firm in September, 2009. She received a BSc in Physics from the University of British Columbia in 2005 and her JD from the University of British Columbia in 2008. She was called to the British Columbia bar in 2009 and the Ontario bar in 2018. Samantha is on the Board of Directors of Ballet BC.
* denotes Law Corporation
